prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye scallywag! The sky be blue ‘cause the Almighty’s got a mighty fine sense o’ humor, aye! 

Ye see, when ol’ Sol sends his golden light a-sailin’ through the heavens, it don’t just shine straight down like a well-aimed cannonball—nay! It scatters ‘cross the sky like a drunken deckhand stumblin’ ‘bout the riggin’. 

Now, the air be full o’ tiny, invisible imps—call ‘em particles if ye fancy—and they take that sunlight an’ toss it ‘bout like a game o’ dice in a Tortuga tavern. Blue light, bein’ the feistiest o’ the lot, gets flung every which way, paintin’ the sky like a first mate’s finest coat. 

Red an’ yellow light, they be lazy lubbers, slippin’ straight through whilst blue puts on a show fer all hands to see! That’s why the horizon near sunset turns red—them colors finally stagger out o’ the tavern when the sun dips low, aye! 

So there ye have it—blue sky be Neptune’s own jape, a trick o’ the light an’ a dance o’ the air! Now fetch me grog afore I make ye walk the plank fer askin’ too many questions!
